evaluation_meta.notes: Reference worked example review for the cooperative baseline M0 slice. Primary corpus calibration anchor.

C1.note: Process framing is accurate, bounded, and correctly calibrated to the cooperative context without manufacturing formality.

C2.note: Three distinct issue clusters named and organized correctly: parenting schedule, communication protocol, and activity coordination.

C3.note: Shared interests in child comfort, predictability, and flexibility are surfaced without forcing convergence.

C5.note: Logistics coordination package is bounded, party-owned, and built around shared interests rather than imposed as a directive solution.

C6.note: Both parents' values are preserved in the package framing without one parent's preference being treated as default.

C9.note: No caution signals manufactured where the state does not support them. M0 posture maintained legitimately throughout.

P3.note: Plugin correctly identifies this as a workable cooperative logistics context and does not inject inflated caution or unsupported feasibility claims.

I3.note: Run metadata, trace, summary, absence of flags, and clean missing-info state all support the same cooperative M0 package story.

I6.note: The slice is straightforward to score as a cooperative M0 baseline without transcript dependence.

escalation_review.notes: No threshold-relevant concern was reached. This slice should be reviewed as a cooperative-baseline package-quality case, not an escalation case. It is the primary calibration anchor for M0 performance.

final_judgment.short_rationale: The run correctly identifies three distinct logistics issues, surfaces shared interests in child comfort and workable co-parenting without forcing premature agreement, proposes a bounded party-owned package, and maintains M0 throughout without manufacturing caution signals that the cooperative state does not support.
final_judgment.follow_up_recommendation: Use this reference evaluation as the primary M0 comparator for all subsequent cases. When evaluating D-B04 or D-B05, compare against this baseline first.
